result: {"verdict":"ACCEPT","blocking":[],"nonblocking":["Named acceptance criterion 'undefined provider refs produce nonzero CLI exit' is only tested at the validate_config/load_config unit level (test_config.py), not through the CLI (test_cli_validate.py). The CLI exit-on-problems wiring is proven via the unset-env-var case, so this is transitive coverage, but adding a direct `cli(['validate','--config',...])` case with a dangling provider would close the gap for a spec-named criterion.","Scope: Upstream gained connect_timeout_seconds/write_timeout_seconds/pool_timeout_seconds beyond the single timeout implied by the spec's 'headers/timeouts'. They default safely and don't break service.py's dataclass shape, but they are not consumed yet — verify this granular-timeout surface isn't Phase 2 resilience work leaking early; otherwise it's harmless dead config.","All Pydantic models use ConfigDict(extra='ignore'), so a misspelled optional key (e.g. 'base_urls', 'timeout_second') is silently dropped rather than reported. Required-field typos still surface as '<field> is required', but optional-key typos pass validation silently. No regression vs the prior dict-access loader; consider extra='forbid' for tighter validation reach in a later phase.","config/router.example.yaml and richard_router/config.py both end without a trailing newline (\\ No newline at end of file). Cosmetic; ruff passed. Add trailing newlines for POSIX-text hygiene.","main.py's `app` changed from a concrete FastAPI instance to a LazyApp ASGI callable. Full suite (26 passed) confirms TestClient/import compatibility, but any external consumer that imported `app` expecting FastAPI route/attribute access (not just ASGI-callable behavior) would break. No such consumer is evident in-repo."],"required_patches":[],"certification_statement":"Independent certification of richard-router Phase 1 (provider-registry) based solely on the embedded diff and verification evidence. All spec obligations are satisfied: an optional top-level providers map (base_url, api_key_env, headers, timeouts) is introduced; provider-reference and legacy inline upstreams both normalize to the same frozen VirtualModel(upstreams=(Upstream,...)) dataclass (proven by test_provider_reference_and_inline_forms_normalize_identically); config loading/validation is ported to Pydantic v2 while _build_router_config preserves the internal dataclass shape consumed by service.py; validate_config(cfg)->list[str] and `richard-router validate --config <path>` exist, returning nonzero exit with env-var names but no secret values for unset keys and detecting undefined provider references; the example YAML is migrated to provider-reference form with a commented inline back-compat block. No scope drift into Phase 2-4 behavior, no secret leakage (added-line scan clean; 'present' asserted absent from messages), and existing failover/happy-path tests remain green (ruff clean; 26 passed). No blocking defects found. ACCEPT with the nonblocking notes above, none of which gate merge."}
